title: aldama latibracteata
intro: Aldama latibracteata, also known as the Mexican tea plant, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Aldama latibracteata include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and its potential to protect against certain diseases. Herbal preparations of Aldama latibracteata can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
